name: Duplicati
description: Encrypted, incremental backups to local, network or cloud storage (S3, B2, WebDAV, SFTP…)

inputs.label: web UI password
inputs.help: Password to sign in to the Duplicati web UI. Stored encrypted.

inputs.label: settings encryption key
inputs.help: Encrypts Duplicati's local settings database — auto-generated if left blank. Stored encrypted.

compose:
name: slashnode-duplicati
services:
  app:
    image: linuxserver/duplicati:2.3.0
    container_name: duplicati
    restart: unless-stopped
    networks:
      - slashnode
    environment:
      PUID: '1000'
      PGID: '1000'
      TZ: Etc/UTC
      SETTINGS_ENCRYPTION_KEY: "${secret.SETTINGS_ENCRYPTION_KEY}"
      DUPLICATI__WEBSERVICE_PASSWORD: "${secret.DUPLICATI__WEBSERVICE_PASSWORD}"
    volumes:
      - app_data-config:/config
      - app_data-backups:/backups
      - app_data-source:/source
    ports:
      - 127.0.0.1:8200:8200
networks:
  slashnode:
    external: true
volumes:
  app_data-config:
    name: slashnode-duplicati_app_data-config
  app_data-backups:
    name: slashnode-duplicati_app_data-backups
  app_data-source:
    name: slashnode-duplicati_app_data-source
notes: Sign in to the web UI with the password you set. Backups are stored in the `backups` volume by default and the `source` volume is what gets backed up — to back up real data, add a host bind-mount (e.g. `- /var/lib/slashnode:/source/slashnode:ro`) or another app's volume under `/source`, then point a Duplicati backup job at it. Destinations can also be remote (S3, Backblaze B2, WebDAV, SFTP, Google Drive…).
